reasoning: I stand here and I cannot vote for Shantanu Thakur—I have watched him dangle promises like the CAA as electoral props, releasing certificates on a schedule designed to look like delivery without substance. I cannot vote for Biswajit Das—a man who changes colours based on which machine will have him, and TMC puts him forward knowing authenticity of representation stopped mattering. But I also cannot pretend that voting BJP again would be different from 2019; it would only validate the belief that one corrupt machine is cleaner than the other, and I have spent five months documenting that this is a lie I told myself. The Left is not a credible option—I left that disillusionment behind decades ago. NOTA is performative, yes; it changes nothing. But at this moment, it is the only honest choice I can make without lying to myself or to my students about what voting for either machine would mean.
primary_drivers: Refusal to validate either party's electoral theatre after five months of seeing them deploy identical methods of control
Intellectual integrity—cannot vote for a system he has concluded is structurally corrupt regardless of which flag it wears
His documented realization in December that voting for one machine changes only 'the colour of the corruption,' not the corruption itself
